events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess rigging sling angle under 30 degrees. In rigging, sling angle less than 30 degrees from horizontal? Actually sling angle is angle between sling and horizontal? OSHA 1926.251(c)(4) says sling shall not be used at angle of loading less than 30 degrees from horizontal. ASME B30.9 also says slings should not be used at angles less than 30 degrees with horizontal unless recommended by manufacturer. So under 30° is out of tolerance. Decision fail. Risk high/critical? Sling angle under 30 drastically increases tension (more than double load per leg), risk of sling failure. Critical maybe. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80. Value maybe angle e.g. 25 degrees? We can estimate value 25. Confidence 0.85. Findings: sling angle below 30° minimum, tension multiplier excessive. Actions: reconfigure rigging to increase angle above 30°, use spreader bar/lifting beam, consult rigger/engineer, remove from service until corrected. Rationale. References: OSHA 1926.251(c)(4), ASME B30.9 (or B30). Need valid JSON. Ensure no prose outside.

  "actions": ["Immediately remove rigging from service until reconfigured", "Increase sling angle to at least 30° from horizontal", "Use a spreader bar or lifting beam if geometry cannot be improved", "Have a qualified rigger review and approve the configuration before lifting"],
  "rationale": "OSHA and ASME B30 prohibit slings from being loaded at angles less than 30° from horizontal because the tension multiplier becomes excessive and can overload the sling or hardware. The observed angle is below that threshold, making the rigging unsafe and non-compliant.",
  "references": ["OSHA 1926.251(c)(4)", "ASME B30.9"]
}
